opp.name: IDEXX March'25
opp.next_step: Send: Share Zycus platform overview tailored to Shawn’s world: Teams integration , AI-driven intake , and how exceptions/approvals can be routed and tracked without extra admin. Enable escalation: Provide a 2–3 slide “forwardable” exec brief Shawn can send to his boss (problem, why now, where it fits alongside/against AAA, what success looks like). Multi-thread: Identify and engage the real owners: Shawn’s boss , plus whoever runs the global procurement platform rollout (Procurement Ops / IT / Transformation). Contract reality check: Confirm AAA contract term/renewal window , and whether IDEXX can run add-on pilots during the contract or only at renewal. Tie to transportation use case: Anchor on Shawn’s scope (international transportation/carrier mgmt): intake for lane/carrier changes, approvals, supplier onboarding, and exception handling. Authority: Shawn = influencer/champion . Final decision sits with his boss + senior management levels . Need: Fit signal for Teams-based intake + exception routing , especially around transportation workflows and global standardization. Budget: Not discussed; likely exists at global program level but may be contract-gated by AAA. Timeline: Not stated; dependent on global rollout gates + AAA contract evaluation window.
opp.ais_why:
• Notes call out a fit signal for Teams-based intake plus AI-driven intake and exception routing tied to Shawn's transportation workflows at IDEXX.
• Champion Shawn is positioned as an influencer needing a forwardable exec brief, but the real decision is gated by an existing AAA contract and global rollout owners.
• Qualified stage with multi-thread strategy being formed; AI interest exists but is contained to one workflow and constrained by competing contract.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial impact was articulated on the 04 Feb 2026 call. Shawn Neal (Corporate International Transportation) manages carrier management and international shipments but did not state cost savings, cycle time reduction, error rates, or operational pain metrics. The call focused on tool capabilities (Teams integration, AI intake) rather than IDEXX's financial or operational drivers. The only qualitative signal is that IDEXX has a global procurement platform initiative intended to optimize operations across regions (Avoma key takeaway, 04 Feb 2026), but no business problem, compelling event, or quantified value case was shared. To build a defensible business case, Zycus must re-engage Shawn and his boss to surface the specific operational pain (e.g. manual carrier onboarding cycle time, exception-handling delays, global standardization gaps) and quantify the impact (cost per manual touchpoint, headcount allocation, error/rework rates). Without this, no Economic Buyer will sponsor the deal.

Avoma action item 04 Feb 2026: 'Shawn Neal will request literature on the procurement tool to share with his boss for further consideration.' (stated intent to champion internally)

champion.narrative: Shawn Neal is a developing champion but has gone silent for 142 days (last contact 04 Feb 2026). On that call he expressed interest in Zycus capabilities (Teams integration, AI intake) relevant to his transportation workflows and requested literature and a forwardable exec brief to escalate internally to his boss. This indicates initial advocacy and willingness to socialize Zycus internally. However, Shawn explicitly stated he is not the decision maker and that the platform decision escalates multiple levels above him, so his influence is limited to his transportation domain. He has no access to the Economic Buyer or global procurement rollout owner confirmed on the call. Since 04 Feb 2026 there has been no follow-up activity, no internal escalation visible, and no reply to the literature/exec brief that was presumably sent (Avoma action item). The 141-day silence suggests the thread has either stalled, been deprioritized, or Shawn was unable to secure internal attention. Shawn's championing strength is weak due to lack of follow-through, no power-role access, and no evidence of active advocacy (no introductions, no internal docs shared, no competitive defense). To re-activate Shawn, Zycus must re-engage with a specific, time-bound ask (e.g. a 15-minute call with Shawn and his boss to validate fit and AAA timing) and confirm whether he is still engaged or if the opportunity has moved to another internal owner (or been abandoned). If Shawn is unresponsive, Zycus must multi-thread directly to higher-level procurement/transformation contacts (Sarah Grassi, Michiel Schreuder, or another global platform owner) using the account-level relationships.

Salesforce Competitors__c: empty (no competitor logged in canonical field)
No other competitive names mentioned in call, Next_Step__c, or task history
competition.narrative: AAA Payout Company is the incumbent and the primary competitive threat. On the 04 Feb 2026 call Shawn Neal confirmed that IDEXX has signed a global S2P contract with AAA covering contracts, pricing, and procurement tools, and that the contract is of unknown duration requiring evaluation before any alternative vendor (including Zycus) can be considered. AAA is not a peer competitor in a bake-off; it is a structural blocker. IDEXX cannot move forward with Zycus unless and until they evaluate the AAA contract (term, renewal window, exit terms, performance gaps) and decide to change vendors. No other competitors (Ariba, Coupa, SAP, GEP, Jaggaer) were mentioned on the call, and the Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. The competitive wedge is the gap between AAA's current-state capabilities and IDEXX's need for Teams integration, AI-driven intake, and global workflow optimization — but that wedge has not been articulated or quantified by the buyer. To win, Zycus must re-engage, confirm the AAA contract timeline and renewal window, surface IDEXX's dissatisfaction or unmet needs with AAA (manual workflows, lack of Teams integration, weak AI, global fragmentation), and position Zycus as the better-fit alternative when the AAA evaluation window opens. If the AAA contract has multiple years remaining with no early exit or pilot flexibility, this deal is not viable in 2026 and the close date must slip to align with the AAA renewal cycle (likely 2027 or later).

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain was articulated by Shawn Neal on the 04 Feb 2026 call. He described his role (Corporate International Transportation, managing carrier management and global shipments) but did not state what is broken, manual, slow, or costly in his current workflows. The call was reactive (Shawn asked 'what does your company do?') rather than pain-driven. The only indirect signal is that IDEXX has a 'global procurement platform initiative intended to optimize operations across regions' (Avoma key takeaway, 04 Feb 2026), implying dissatisfaction with current-state fragmentation or inefficiency, but no pain owner, quantified impact, or compelling event was named. Shawn did express interest in Teams integration and AI-driven intake for exception handling and approvals in his transportation workflows, suggesting he sees value in automation, but he did not describe the current manual burden, error rates, or cycle time delays that would justify a platform change. To surface pain, Zycus must re-engage Shawn and his boss with discovery questions: what manual touchpoints exist in carrier onboarding, lane changes, exception routing, and approval workflows today? How much time does the team spend on these? What errors or delays occur? What is the cost of the current fragmented state across regions? Who owns this pain at the executive level (Procurement, Supply Chain, Transformation)? Without confirmed pain and a pain owner, no Economic Buyer will sponsor the business case.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec, and procurement channel have not been discussed. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, or legal redline process has been addressed. The 04 Feb 2026 call was exploratory and never reached commercial or contracting topics. The AAA Payout Company contract is a structural dependency: IDEXX cannot move forward with Zycus until the AAA contract term, renewal window, and exit terms are understood, and it is unclear whether IDEXX can run an add-on pilot during the AAA contract or only at renewal. No SI/partner involvement has been mentioned (though Zycus may need an implementation partner for a global S2P rollout of this scale). No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is available in Salesforce, so the AAA contract is a forcing function with an unknown timeline. To establish the paper process, Zycus must re-engage, confirm the AAA contract details (term, renewal, exit terms, pilot flexibility), map the IDEXX legal/procurement/InfoSec stakeholders who will review any Zycus MSA/SOW, and define the contracting path (legal review duration, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement approval, implementation scoping).

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. On the 04 Feb 2026 call Shawn Neal explicitly stated that procurement platform vendor decisions escalate beyond him to 'my immediate boss' and 'additional senior management levels' (Avoma, 04 Feb 2026), and that the AAA contract and global rollout are managed at a corporate level requiring 'approval from several management levels beyond Shawn's scope'. Shawn is an influencer in Corporate International Transportation but owns no budget authority for a platform decision. The true Economic Buyer is likely at VP or C-level Procurement (Sarah Grassi, Director of Global Procurement, is a known account contact but not confirmed as EB and has never been engaged on this opportunity), or within the Transformation/Procurement Ops function managing the global rollout. Zero contact roles exist on this opportunity, and no EB-level conversation has occurred. The 141-day silence means no progress has been made toward mapping or reaching the EB. To advance, Zycus must re-engage Shawn to secure an introduction to his boss and the global platform owner, confirm who controls the budget and AAA contract evaluation, and bring that person into a discovery conversation with economic and strategic framing.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but not formalized. Shawn Neal confirmed on 04 Feb 2026 that any procurement platform decision must escalate from him to his immediate boss and then to 'additional senior management levels', and that the AAA contract and global rollout are managed at a corporate/executive level. The call sequence to date is a single exploratory conversation with a mid-level transportation manager; no senior stakeholders, procurement leadership, IT/Transformation, or executive sponsors have joined. IDEXX is under contract with the relevant stakeholder for global S2P (contracts, pricing, procurement tools), and any platform change requires evaluating that contract (term, renewal window, exit terms) before Zycus can be considered. The approval chain is multi-level but not mapped (Shawn's boss is unnamed, the global rollout owner is unknown, and no EB/CFO approval path has been discussed). No formal evaluation stage gates (RFI shortlist, RFP scoring, finalist selection, EB proposal, contract negotiation) have been defined. To clarify the process, Zycus must re-engage Shawn, map the approval chain (his boss, the global platform owner, Procurement leadership, IT/Transformation, Finance/EB), confirm the AAA contract evaluation timeline, and define the steps from re-engagement to decision (discovery, demo, RFI/RFP if required, pricing, EB business case, contract).

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP requirements, or decision framework has been shared. The 04 Feb 2026 call was introductory: Shawn asked 'what does your company do?' and Zycus provided a capability overview (source-to-pay suite, Teams front-end, AI intake, S/4 HANA integration). Shawn expressed interest in Teams integration and AI-driven intake for his transportation workflows (carrier management, exception handling) but did not articulate IDEXX's technical requirements, evaluation scorecard, or must-haves vs nice-to-haves. No RFI, RFP, or BRD (Business Requirements Document) has been issued. The call history shows no use-case validation, demo, or structured evaluation session. To surface decision criteria, Zycus must re-engage and run a discovery session with Shawn and his boss to map IDEXX's platform requirements (Teams integration depth, AI use cases, S/4 HANA connector scope, global rollout phasing, contract flexibility, ROI benchmarks) and confirm how the decision will be scored (technical fit, pricing, incumbent comparison, change-management risk, reference customers).

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS (AI Score) recorded in Salesforce. On the 04 Feb 2026 call Shawn Neal expressed interest in Zycus Teams integration and AI-driven intake for transportation workflows (carrier management, exception routing, approvals), suggesting appetite for AI-powered automation. However, AI needs were not quantified, and no broader IDEXX stakeholder (EB, global platform owner) has validated AI appetite. AI fit signal is POSITIVE but UNCONFIRMED at the decision-maker level. Classify as AI Curious based on Shawn's interest, but true tier (AI Hungry vs Curious vs Resistant) cannot be assessed without EB/DM engagement and confirmation of AI prioritization in IDEXX's global platform initiative.

He expressed initial interest and willingness to escalate Zycus internally on 04 Feb 2026, but has gone silent for 142 days with no follow-up, no introductions, and no evidence of internal advocacy. He is a mid-level influencer (Corporate International Transportation) with no budget authority or power-role access, and the deal decision escalates multiple levels above him. Single-threaded dependency on Shawn creates high ghost risk.

Stage (Qualified) and forecast (Pipeline) imply early exploration, but the deal has stalled before any meaningful next step: no demos scheduled, no RFI/RFP round, no stakeholder expansion, no AAA contract intelligence gathered.

Close date 31 Dec 2026 (188 days out) is not credible without immediate re-engagement, multi-threading to power, AAA evaluation timeline confirmation, and a full enterprise sales cycle (discovery depth, demos, RFI/shortlist, commercials, EB proposal, contracting) — realistically 9–12 months from re-start.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dead 141 days after single exploratory call with no follow-up, no power engagement, no EB mapped, and AAA incumbent contract blocking. Close date requires 9+ months of work not yet started; zero momentum.

flags: Single-threaded through mid-level influencer (Shawn Neal, Corporate International Transportation) with no access to Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or global platform owner; 141-day silence suggests thread has stalled or been deprioritized. Multi-thread to Shawn's boss and procurement leadership (Sarah Grassi, Michiel Schreuder, or transformation owner) to secure power-role engagement and validate deal viability.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold (141 days dark) and contract-gated by AAA incumbent. Immediate actions: (1) Re-engage Shawn with time-bound ask to validate whether opportunity is still live and secure introduction to his boss; (2) Multi-thread to account-level procurement/transformation contacts (Sarah Grassi, Michiel Schreuder) to bypass single-thread risk; (3) Confirm AAA contract timeline to assess deal viability in 2026 vs 2027+.

competitive_position.summary: AAA Payout Company is the sole known competitor and the primary current threat. AAA is the incumbent, holding a signed global S2P contract (contracts, pricing, procurement tools) of unknown term and renewal date. IDEXX cannot proceed with Zycus until the AAA contract is evaluated (term, performance, exit terms). No peer competitors (Ariba, Coupa, GEP, SAP, Jaggaer) have been named. The competitive threat is structural (contract lock-in) rather than feature/price-based. AAA's threat level is HIGH because the contract blocks near-term movement, but it may DECLINE if IDEXX identifies gaps in AAA's capabilities (no Teams integration, weak AI, global workflow limitations) and the contract renewal window opens within a realistic timeframe. The single strongest current threat is the AAA incumbent contract, which gates all forward progress until IDEXX evaluates it and decides to change vendors.

competitors.name: AAA Payout Company
competitors.quote: We just signed on with another company. I'm not sure how long that's for, but we have elected AAA payout company, and they kinda do the same thing for the most part. They oversee the contract and the pricing as well as any procurement tools that we may have brought on board, and this isn't just a US thing. I think it's a global thing.
competitors.how_we_win: Confirm AAA contract term and renewal window; surface IDEXX's unmet needs (Teams integration, AI-driven intake, global workflow optimization, carrier management automation) that AAA does not deliver; position Zycus Teams front-end and AI capabilities as the better-fit alternative; secure discovery session with Shawn's boss and global platform owner when AAA evaluation window opens; if AAA contract has multi-year term with no exit flexibility, align Zycus close date to AAA renewal cycle (likely 2027+).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was strong on the 04 Feb 2026 call: Zycus emphasized Teams integration, AI-driven intake, conversational interaction, and the move from visionary to leader in Gartner based on AI innovation. Shawn expressed interest in Teams/AI for his transportation workflows (exception handling, approvals). However, no AIS score is recorded in Salesforce, and the deal has been dark for 142 days, so AI positioning has not been validated beyond the initial conversation or converted into a formal evaluation. AI fit signal is positive but unconfirmed at the buyer's executive/EB level.

items.quote: We utilize Teams as our front end. So in other words, if I need to requisition something or check on the status of a supplier, ask for a new supplier, anything that goes on in your global procurement pursuit, we utilize Teams as that point of entry. [...] You guys use Teams for your internal collaboration? Yeah. For the most part. Yeah.
items.source: Avoma call 04 Feb 2026, Zycus rep and Shawn Neal
items.criterion: Teams integration for intake and orchestration

items.quote: We have an advantage in terms of technology, and the Teams front end [...] we utilize Teams as our front end. [...] All your end users can literally just have a conversational interaction with all the procurement backbones. So that's been sort of a revolutionary entrance into the procure tech space, and I think it's why we went from a visionary position this year to a leadership position because of our innovation around AI and intake.
We have an open connector with a built in integration to S/4 HANA. I believe that's the platform you guys use for your backbone ERP.
There is an initiative for the company that we use to be used globally. Just something to optimize the company.
items.source: Avoma call 04 Feb 2026, Shawn Neal (strategic intent stated, but IDEXX requirements and Zycus fit not validated)
items.criterion: Global procurement platform for standardization and optimization

customer_expectations_fit.summary: IDEXX expressed interest in Teams integration and AI-driven intake for transportation workflows (carrier management, exception handling, approvals) on the 04 Feb 2026 call, and Zycus capabilities align well with that need. However, broader IDEXX requirements for a global S2P platform rollout (technical fit, contract flexibility, pricing, ROI, change management, global deployment phasing) have not been discussed, so fit beyond the Teams/AI dimension is unconfirmed. The opportunity is early-stage and under-qualified.

hard.ais_why:
• Notes call out a fit signal for Teams-based intake plus AI-driven intake and exception routing tied to Shawn's transportation workflows at IDEXX.
• Champion Shawn is positioned as an influencer needing a forwardable exec brief, but the real decision is gated by an existing AAA contract and global rollout owners.
• Qualified stage with multi-thread strategy being formed; AI interest exists but is contained to one workflow and constrained by competing contract.
hard.next_step:
• Send: Share Zycus platform overview tailored to Shawn’s world: Teams integration, AI-driven intake, and how exceptions/approvals can be routed and tracked without extra admin.
  • Enable escalation: Provide a 2–3 slide “forwardable” exec brief Shawn can send to his boss (problem, why now, where it fits alongside/against AAA, what success looks like).
  • Multi-thread: Identify and engage the real owners: Shawn’s boss, plus whoever runs the global procurement platform rollout (Procurement Ops / IT / Transformation).
  • Contract reality check: Confirm AAA contract term/renewal window, and whether IDEXX can run add-on pilots during the contract or only at renewal.
  • Tie to transportation use case: Anchor on Shawn’s scope (international transportation/carrier mgmt): intake for lane/carrier changes, approvals, supplier onboarding, and exception handling.

 

  • Authority: Shawn = influencer/champion. Final decision sits with his boss + senior management levels.
  • Need: Fit signal for Teams-based intake + exception routing, especially around transportation workflows and global standardization.
  • Budget: Not discussed; likely exists at global program level but may be contract-gated by AAA.
  • Timeline: Not stated; dependent on global rollout gates + AAA contract evaluation window.
